world_date: Month of the Ashen Dreaming, Year 1 of the Hollow Accord, Day 15
major_event: Varek emerged from below the sealed level before dawn — alone, silent, eyes changed. He carried the iron case. It was sealed. He said nothing to Kessa Stonebraid, who had not moved. He walked past her without stopping. Ironmaul was not at the threshold. The second door was not yet finished. Varek walked into the garrison and asked for water.

primary_event.name: Varek Ascends — The Iron Case Returns With Him
primary_event.summary: Grand Druid Varek emerged before dawn after more than a full day below. He carried the iron case, sealed. He said nothing. His eyes were wrong — not vacant, not afraid, but altered in a way Kessa Stonebraid could not name. He asked only for water. The Monastery instruction rider is still a day away.

supporting_events.name: Admiral Sends the Word — Retainer Clause Triggered
supporting_events.summary: Before midday, the Admiral dictated a short declaration to his personal scribe, not his secretary of state. It named Caeris Thorne as a destabilizing claimant and requested Dur Khadur's 'advisory presence' under the existing retainer. The dream is still in him. The prophet's reading is its mouth.

supporting_events.name: Caeris Opens the Inner Document — The Gnome Offer Revealed
supporting_events.summary: Caeris Thorne opened the Gnome inner document at midday. It named four individuals who already hold knowledge of the proclamation, including a Vampire operative and a Tidefall court agent. Caeris read it twice, stood, and went to his father's room for the first time in three days.

supporting_events.name: Dur Khadur Staging Unit Begins Moving — Retainer Clause Active
supporting_events.summary: By afternoon the Dur Khadur coastal advance unit received the Admiral's sealed declaration and began slow forward movement from their staging camp toward the Twin Cities harbor approach. They move under the legal fiction of advisory transit. Twelve miles becomes eight.

active_events.name: Varek Ascends — The Iron Case Returns With Him
active_events.summary: Varek emerged before dawn carrying the iron case, sealed. His eyes were altered. He said nothing and asked only for water. Kessa Stonebraid watched him pass. Ironmaul does not yet know he has emerged. The Monastery instruction rider is still a day out.
active_events.consequences: When Ironmaul learns Varek has returned, he will face the man — and whatever the man has become. The iron case's second seal must be confirmed. If the case was opened and re-sealed, something may have passed between Varek and whatever was inside.

active_events.name: Retainer Clause Triggered — Dur Khadur Moves on Twin Cities
active_events.summary: The Admiral's declaration reached Dur Khadur's advance unit commander by midday. The staging unit began forward movement. Eight miles from the harbor approach by nightfall. The Twin Cities court has not yet received word of the declaration.
active_events.consequences: The Twin Cities will learn of the declaration through secondary channels — likely Vampire or Gnome intelligence — within 1 tick. When they do, the political crisis accelerates into a potential military confrontation.

active_events.name: Caeris Moves Toward His Father — Proclamation Clock Running
active_events.summary: The Gnome inner document broke Caeris's paralysis. He walked to his father's chambers for the first time in days. What he said there is unknown. Whether Roderic was lucid enough to receive it is unknown. The proclamation remains unsigned but Caeris is now in motion.
active_events.consequences: If Roderic signs before word of the Dur Khadur movement reaches the city, Caeris may be able to control the narrative. If not, the arrival of Dur Khadur forces will define the succession on military rather than legal terms.

active_events.name: Dur Khadur Forward Unit — Eight Miles and Closing
active_events.summary: The advance unit moved from twelve miles to eight miles from the harbor approach under advisory transit fiction. The unit commander is experienced and will not enter the city without a second authorization, but the posture is unmistakably forward.
active_events.consequences: At current pace, the unit could reach city outskirts within 2 ticks. The Twin Cities garrison has not yet been placed on elevated readiness. The gap between awareness and response is the most dangerous interval.

active_events.name: Rowen — The Ruin Below the Surface
active_events.summary: The fleet is within 1-2 ticks of the coastal ruin. Rowen is navigating to something below the waterline. His navigator confirmed the depth-line marks on the old chart; the ruin has two layers — one visible at low tide, one submerged. Rowen has not explained why this matters.
active_events.consequences: Arrival at the ruin will require diving operations or shallow-draft boats. Whatever Rowen found in the ancient document involves the submerged layer. The ruin may be connected to the sinking island's geography.

active_events.name: Seer in Transit — Day Two, Road South
active_events.summary: The Seer spent the day on the harbor road south. He passed two waypost villages and stopped at a roadside inn before dusk. A traveling merchant mentioned the Tidefall council had been 'quieter than usual' for three days. The Seer noted it and walked on.
active_events.consequences: The Seer will arrive in Tidefall in 1 more tick. He will arrive after the Admiral's declaration has already been made. He cannot prevent what is coming — only witness it.

active_events.name: Velorn Clan Consolidation — Shadow Court Fracture
active_events.summary: Velorn's third minor house secured. Lythara's Nythariel envoy is still a day from the Twin Cities. In her absence, Velorn held a quiet clan gathering framed as a seasonal rite. Arkenor's lieutenant attended without invitation from Lythara.
active_events.consequences: The gathering is a declaration dressed as ceremony. When Lythara's attention returns inward she will find the fracture has become a coalition.

active_events.name: Vilefin Goblins — Tinker Informant Approaches Lostfeld
active_events.summary: The tinker-informant is now within half a day of Lostfeld's outer gate. He has already noted the absence of supply traffic on the road. He does not yet know Varek has emerged. He will arrive in time to observe the aftermath.
active_events.consequences: Whatever the informant observes at the gate will form the intelligence package sold to the highest bidder. Varek's emergence — and the iron case — may be visible from the gate if the garrison does not control access quickly.

faction_actions.action: Ironmaul was woken before dawn by a garrison guard who reported Varek walking through the lower corridor asking for water. Ironmaul dressed without speaking and went down. He found Varek seated at the garrison table, the iron case on the floor beside him, sealed. Ironmaul sat across from him and said nothing for a long time. He has sent Kessa Stonebraid outside and posted guards at both entrances. Cost: 2 influence (garrison control), 1 gold.
faction_actions.reason: Ironmaul does not know what Varek encountered below. He knows the case came back sealed. He is watching the man for signs before he asks any question. The second door construction has been quietly paused.
faction_actions.target: Varek — direct observation, controlled garrison access, no outside word yet

faction_actions.action: The instruction rider pressed hard through the night and is now within a day of Lostfeld. He does not know Varek has already emerged. The Stonebreak council received no communication from Varek and is beginning to consider sending a second senior druid northward as observer and potential override authority. Cost: 3 gold (continued rider upkeep, council session), 2 influence.
faction_actions.reason: The silence from Varek for over two days now has tipped the Monastery from concern into controlled fear. They do not know whether to hope he followed the original protocol or dread that he improved on it.
faction_actions.target: Lostfeld — instruction rider, secondary druid observer preparation

faction_actions.action: The Admiral's scribe delivered the sealed declaration by personal courier to the Dur Khadur advance unit commander at the staging camp by late morning. The Admiral then dismissed all remaining council staff and has not been seen since. The prophet visited the study door twice and received no answer. Cost: 3 gold (courier, dismissal payments), 4 influence.
faction_actions.reason: The Admiral made his decision in the smallest, most private way possible — no secretary of state, no council, no formal record. The dream and the pressure met in the locked room and produced a whisper instead of a proclamation. The whisper reached Dur Khadur.
faction_actions.target: Dur Khadur advance unit — sealed retainer activation declaration

faction_actions.action: The advance unit commander read the Admiral's declaration, confirmed the legal threshold under the retainer clause, and ordered measured forward movement: four miles toward the Twin Cities harbor approach before making camp. The unit moves as advisors, not soldiers — no banners, reduced armor visible. Cost: 5 military (unit movement, upkeep), 3 gold.
faction_actions.reason: The clause is active. The commander's orders are to arrive at the city perimeter within advisory norms — close enough to matter, ambiguous enough to be denied. The Dark Council has not yet been formally informed; the commander is operating on pre-issued standing orders.
faction_actions.target: Twin Cities harbor approach — advisory forward movement, four miles advanced

faction_actions.action: Caeris entered his father's chambers with the Gnome document folded inside his coat. Roderic was awake, seated by the window, and lucid enough to recognize him. Caeris spoke for twenty minutes. He did not show the document. He asked his father a single question at the end that the attending physician later described as 'the kind of question you ask when you already know the answer.' Roderic reached for his pen. Cost: 4 influence (direct political engagement), 2 gold.
faction_actions.reason: The Gnome document told Caeris that waiting was no longer available as a strategy. He is not acting from confidence — he is acting because the alternative is being acted upon.
faction_actions.target: King Roderic — direct succession conversation, possible proclamation signing

faction_actions.action: The Tidefall court agent confirmed receipt of the partial eclipse-succession framing offer but has not responded with gold yet. The Gnome courier in the Twin Cities noted unusual guard activity near the palace wing where Caeris's chambers are located. A third coded dispatch was prepared — not sent yet — addressed to the Shadow Court's shadow-envoy currently en route, offering to sell the Tidefall declaration for advance consideration. Cost: 3 gold (courier network upkeep, preparation), 2 influence.
faction_actions.reason: The Gnomes know something significant has moved in the Twin Cities. They are preparing to monetize the Dur Khadur movement before most factions know it has begun. The Shadow Court envoy is the nearest mobile buyer.
faction_actions.target: Shadow Court envoy — pre-positioned offer on Tidefall declaration intelligence

faction_actions.action: The Vampire operative received a private signal from the palace physician's household — Roderic was visited by Caeris and asked for his pen. The operative composed a brief, urgent dispatch to the Shadow Court: 'The old king's hand may move before the sun sets. Position accordingly.' It was carried by a private rider on the Shadow Road. Cost: 5 influence (Shadow Road activation), 2 gold.
faction_actions.reason: The Vampires want the Shadow Court in motion before Caeris can consolidate. A Lythara-backed Caeris is a liability. An isolated Caeris is more useful. They are accelerating multiple actors simultaneously.
faction_actions.target: Shadow Court — urgent dispatch on Roderic's possible signing

faction_actions.action: The Nythariel shadow-envoy arrived at the Twin Cities outer district by late afternoon and secured lodging at a merchant house. He has not yet presented himself at the palace. Lythara received the Vampire dispatch via Shadow Road by evening and immediately sent a coded instruction to the envoy: 'Do not wait for morning. Seek Caeris tonight.' Cost: 6 gold (Shadow Road reply, envoy instructions), 4 influence.
faction_actions.reason: Lythara now knows Roderic may sign the proclamation without her involvement. Her window for positioning herself as a stabilizing voice is hours, not days. The envoy must make contact before the document is signed or the signing becomes public.
faction_actions.target: Twin Cities — Nythariel envoy instructed to seek Caeris Thorne tonight

faction_actions.action: The Wayfarer trader at the neutral waypoint between the Twin Cities and Glenhaven sent back a report: unusual rider traffic on the harbor road south of the city, and one report of Dur Khadur livery spotted at a roadside post — unconfirmed. Elowen received this after midday and convened a brief closed council. She ordered the Tidefall diplomatic inquiry upgraded to a formal envoy request. Cost: 2 gold (council, upgraded dispatch), 2 influence.
faction_actions.reason: Elowen does not know about the retainer clause or the Admiral's declaration, but Dur Khadur livery near the Twin Cities road is not a subtle signal. She is upgrading her intelligence posture without committing to a position.
faction_actions.target: Tidefall — formal envoy request; Wayfarer intelligence upgraded

faction_actions.action: Farrock's commander received Ironmaul's private emergency protocol message by mid-morning: 'If we go silent, do not come. Send word south instead.' The commander read it three times and convened a private meeting with his two senior captains. He confirmed the protocol and identified 'south' as meaning both the Monastery at Stonebreak and the Twin Cities. He made no other action. Cost: 1 gold (senior captain meeting).
faction_actions.reason: The protocol from Ironmaul is alarming precisely because it is so quiet. A man who says 'do not come' is a man who expects whatever is coming to be contagious. Farrock will watch and wait but has now defined its emergency response.
faction_actions.target: Emergency protocol acknowledged — Twin Cities and Monastery identified as southern recipients

faction_actions.action: The tinker-informant reached Lostfeld's outer road by late afternoon. He noted: no supply carts in two days confirmed, one additional garrison guard post at the lower outer gate, and a dwarf smith working on what appeared to be a large iron frame in the outer yard — visible from the road. He made no contact and will complete his observation by morning. Cost: 1 gold (supplies, continued cover).
faction_actions.reason: The informant is professional. He records what he sees without triggering alertness. The iron frame is significant enough to note specifically.
faction_actions.target: Lostfeld outer gate — continued observation, iron frame noted, no contact

faction_actions.action: The junior apprentice shaman reached a hill overlooking the Twin Cities southern road by midday. He observed a group of riders moving in formation — not merchants, not postal — heading south at measured military pace. He could not identify livery from his distance. He prepared a bead-message for return to the Gilgeth elder council. Cost: 1 gold (apprentice upkeep).
faction_actions.reason: The sky god is aiming at something. The apprentice has found movement. He does not know enough to name it but he knows it is not ordinary traffic.
faction_actions.target: Gilgeth — bead-message return: unusual military formation moving south from Twin Cities

faction_actions.action: By mid-morning the fleet's lookout spotted the coastal ruin — a broken tower and collapsed seawall just visible above the tide line, with submerged stonework extending further than the tower's footprint suggested. Rowen ordered anchor. He spent two hours studying the site from the deck before calling for a shallow-draft rowing boat. He descended himself, unannounced, with the navigator and one trusted crew member. Cost: 2 military (anchor watch, reduced patrol), 1 gold.
faction_actions.reason: Rowen does not delegate what the document pointed him toward. The submerged layer is the destination. Whatever the ancient text described is below the waterline at this site.
faction_actions.target: Coastal ruin — first approach, Rowen descending in shallow-draft boat personally

recent_events.text: Varek emerged before dawn carrying the iron case, sealed. His eyes were changed. He asked only for water. Ironmaul found him at the garrison table and has sealed the room. Kessa Stonebraid was sent outside. No word has left the mountain yet.

The Admiral's sealed declaration was delivered to Dur Khadur's advance commander by personal courier before noon. The Admiral has not been seen since. His prophet visited the study door twice and received no answer. The retainer clause is now active.

Caeris entered his father's chambers and spoke for twenty minutes. Roderic reached for his pen. A Nythariel shadow-envoy is already in the city's outer district, now under orders to find Caeris before morning. The Dur Khadur unit is eight miles from the harbor approach.

Rowen's fleet anchored off the coastal ruin. The submerged stonework extends beyond the visible tower. Rowen descended in a shallow-draft boat with the navigator and one crew member. He has not explained what he is looking for.

The Vilefin tinker-informant confirmed no supply traffic for two days, observed an additional garrison post, and spotted an iron frame being worked in the outer yard. The goblin intelligence picture is nearly complete enough to sell.

active_tensions.factions: Twin Cities — Dur Khadur Advance vs Proclamation Race
active_tensions.description: Dur Khadur is eight miles from the harbor approach and closing. Caeris may have just moved Roderic toward signing. The race is between a legal document and a foreign military unit using the fiction of advisory transit. Neither side knows the other's exact timing.

active_tensions.factions: Lostfeld — Varek's Return and the Iron Case
active_tensions.description: Varek is back. The case came back with him, sealed. But his eyes are wrong and he has said nothing. Ironmaul must now decide whether to ask the question or let the silence answer itself. The Monastery instruction rider arrives tomorrow with orders that may already be obsolete.

active_tensions.factions: Shadow Court — Lythara Racing the Signing
active_tensions.description: The Nythariel envoy has been ordered to find Caeris tonight. Lythara is trying to insert herself into the succession before the document is signed. But the Vampires have already dispatched intelligence to her, and the Gnomes are trying to sell her the Tidefall declaration. Too many vectors converging on the same night.

The Admiral has not been seen since sending the declaration. His prophet cannot reach him. The council is unaware the clause was triggered. A city's naval leadership made a decision that moves foreign troops, and the city itself does not know it yet.

character_updates.name: Grand Druid Varek
character_updates.status: Emerged before dawn carrying the sealed iron case. Eyes visibly altered. Has said nothing beyond asking for water. Seated in the garrison under Ironmaul's silent observation. The Monastery instruction has not yet reached him.

character_updates.name: Thane Ironmaul
character_updates.status: Found Varek at the garrison table before dawn. Has sealed the room, sent Kessa outside, posted guards. Is watching Varek without yet asking questions. Second iron door construction quietly paused.

character_updates.name: Admiral (Tidefall)
character_updates.status: Sent the retainer activation declaration by personal scribe-courier. Has not been seen since. Prophet cannot reach him. Council is unaware the clause was triggered. Psychologically isolated.

character_updates.name: Caeris Thorne
character_updates.status: Entered his father's chambers. Roderic reached for his pen. Shadow Court envoy is in the city and approaching. Dur Khadur unit is eight miles away. Caeris is now racing on all three tracks simultaneously without knowing it.

character_updates.name: Rowen
character_updates.status: Anchored at the coastal ruin. Descended personally in a shallow-draft boat with navigator and one crew. Investigating submerged layer. Has not explained the purpose to the rest of the fleet.

character_updates.name: Queen Lythara
character_updates.status: Received Vampire dispatch via Shadow Road — Roderic may sign before sunset. Has ordered Nythariel envoy to seek Caeris tonight. Velorn's coalition gathering is still invisible to her.

faction_morale.reason: Varek returned but the return brought no relief — only a changed man and a sealed case and a garrison sealed from outside. The second door construction paused mid-frame. The garrison is holding very still.

The Admiral has vanished into his study after triggering a foreign military movement. His council does not know what he did. The prophet is standing at a locked door. The city's most powerful institution has no visible head.

Roderic may be signing. Dur Khadur is eight miles out. A shadow envoy is in the outer district. The city does not yet know any of this — but the palace does, in pieces, and the pieces are not fitting together into reassurance.

The clause triggered. The unit is moving under legitimate cover. The Dark Council's investment in patience is paying forward. They have legal fiction and military position and the Admiral has gone silent — ideal conditions.

Lythara is racing events she did not initiate and cannot fully see. Velorn's coalition grows. The envoy is rushing toward a conversation that may already have concluded. She is behind in three separate plays.

Varek emerged and the instruction rider does not know it yet. The council's fear has deepened — they sent orders for a man who has already returned from wherever their orders were trying to prevent him going.

Caeris opened the inner document. The Dur Khadur movement is beginning. The Gnomes are pre-positioning to sell the declaration intelligence to the Shadow Court envoy. Every actor is moving and the Gnomes know it before most actors do.

Two dispatches sent, both landed with impact. Caeris is moving. Lythara is accelerating. The Vampires have spent influence efficiently and are watching three separate crises develop in directions they seeded.

faction_resources.pressure: Gold draining from security operations and Caeris's political movement. Influence softening further from visible palace tension.
Gold steady but influence dropping sharply from Admiral's isolation and invisible retainer activation. Military slightly reduced.
Shadow Road activation expensive. Gold and influence draining under racing-event pressure. Velorn consolidation invisible but real drain.
Small continued drain from upgraded envoy request and council session. Influence holding stable.
Gold critically low. Second door paused but materials already spent. Military further reduced. Crisis threshold passed.
Courier network upkeep steady. Third dispatch prepared but not sent. Influence dominant.
Gold approaching low threshold. Continued rider and council costs. Influence holding but fear-current accelerating.
Food slow decline at sea. Anchor and rowing boat operations minor cost. Rowen's absence from deck noted by senior crew.
Forward unit movement costly. Military reduced from active deployment. Gold draining at accelerated pace.
Minimal cost. Emergency protocol acknowledged and filed. Stable and watchful.
Informant upkeep minimal. Intelligence package nearing completion. Ready to monetize within 1 tick.
Shadow Road and dual dispatch costly in influence. Gold stable. Well-positioned.
Gold and influence low. Apprentice return upkeep minimal. Watching.
Caravan opium trade destabilizing southern supply lines. Food slow decline. Gold low.
Stable in Dragonscar Peaks. Observing eclipse omens from isolation. No action taken.

seer_state.belief_about_user: The presence beyond the veil is not fully understood.

seer_journey.purpose: Seer in Transit — Day Two, Road South
seer_journey.last_outcome: The Seer spent the day on the harbor road south. He passed two waypost villages and stopped at a roadside inn before dusk. A traveling merchant mentioned the Tidefall council had been 'quieter than usual' for three days. The Seer noted it and walked on.
